Hierarchy

Ancestors

  • WPEDisplay
  • GObject

Constructors

wpe_display_headless_new

Create a new WPEDisplayHeadless.

since: 2.0

wpe_display_headless_new_for_device

Create a new WPEDisplayHeadless for the DRM device with name.

since: 2.0

Instance methods

Methods inherited from WPEDisplay (18)
wpe_display_connect

Connect the display to the native system.

since: 2.0

wpe_display_create_gamepad_manager

Create a WPEGamepadManager to handle gamepads.

since: 2.0

wpe_display_create_toplevel

Create a new WPEToplevel on display with max_views allowed. The parameter max_views will be ignored if display doesn’t support multiple views per toplevel. This function returns NULL if the platform implementation doesn’t support explicit creation of WPEToplevel.

since: 2.0

wpe_display_disconnected

Emit the signal WPEDisplay::disconnected with the given error.

since: 2.0

wpe_display_get_available_input_devices

Get the available input devices of display that can be used by creators to adjust their UI based on the available interactions.

since: 2.0

wpe_display_get_clipboard

Get the WPEClipboard of display. If the platform doesn’t support clipboard, a local WPEClipboard is created.

since: 2.0

wpe_display_get_drm_device

Get the DRM device of display. This is the main device that will be used to initialize the EGL display and allocate GBM buffers by default. The DRM device required to allocate GBM buffers for direct scanout will be set as main or group device in WPEBufferFormats.

since: 2.0

wpe_display_get_egl_display

Get the EGLDisplay of display.

since: 2.0

wpe_display_get_keymap

Get the WPEKeymap of display.

since: 2.0

wpe_display_get_n_screens

Get the number of screens of display.

since: 2.0

wpe_display_get_preferred_buffer_formats

Get the list of preferred buffer formats for display.

since: 2.0

wpe_display_get_screen

Get the screen of display at index.

since: 2.0

wpe_display_get_settings

Get the WPESettings of display.

since: 2.0

wpe_display_screen_added

Emit the signal WPEDisplay::screen-added.

since: 2.0

wpe_display_screen_removed

Emit the signal WPEDisplay::screen-removed. Note that wpe_screen_invalidate() is called before the signal is emitted.

since: 2.0

wpe_display_set_available_input_devices

Sets the available input devices for a display.

since: 2.0

wpe_display_set_primary

Set display as the primary display.

since: 2.0

wpe_display_use_explicit_sync

Get whether explicit sync should be used with display for supported buffers.

since: 2.0

Please see GObject for a full list of methods.

Properties

Properties inherited from WPEDisplay (1)
WPEPlatform.Display:available-input-devices

The input devices (e.g. mouse, keyboard or touchscreen) available to use for this display.

since: 2.0

Signals

Signals inherited from WPEDisplay (3)
WPEDisplay::disconnected

Emitted when the display is disconnected from the platform display.

since: 2.0

WPEDisplay::screen-added

Emitted when a screen is added.

since: 2.0

WPEDisplay::screen-removed

Emitted after a screen is removed. Note that the screen is always invalidated before this signal is emitted.

since: 2.0

Signals inherited from GObject (1)
GObject::notify

The notify signal is emitted on an object when one of its properties has its value set through g_object_set_property(), g_object_set(), et al.

since: 2.0
